---
title: "EpiFlow D3 — Quick Start"
subtitle: "Get from data to results in 10 minutes"
author: "Serrano Lab · Boston University"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    documentclass: article
    geometry: 
      - margin=0.8in
      - top=0.9in
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{fontspec}
        \renewcommand{\familydefault}{\sfdefault}
        \IfFontExistsTF{Helvetica Neue}{
          \setsansfont{Helvetica Neue}[Scale = 0.95]
          \setmainfont{Helvetica Neue}[Scale = 0.95]
        }{
          \IfFontExistsTF{Arial}{
            \setsansfont{Arial}[Scale = 0.95]
            \setmainfont{Arial}[Scale = 0.95]
          }{
            \usepackage{helvet}
            \renewcommand{\familydefault}{\sfdefault}
          }
        }
        \setmonofont{Courier New}[Scale=0.85]

        \usepackage{fancyhdr}
        \pagestyle{fancy}
        \fancyhf{}
        \fancyhead[L]{\footnotesize\color{gray}\textbf{EPIFLOW D3 — QUICK START}}
        \fancyhead[R]{\footnotesize\color{gray}Serrano Lab}
        \fancyfoot[C]{\footnotesize\color{gray}\thepage}
        \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0.5pt}
        \setlength{\headheight}{14pt}

        \usepackage[table,dvipsnames]{xcolor}
        \definecolor{oiblue}{HTML}{0072B2}
        \definecolor{oivermillion}{HTML}{D55E00}
        \definecolor{oigreen}{HTML}{009E73}
        \definecolor{oiorange}{HTML}{E69F00}
        \definecolor{oiskyblue}{HTML}{56B4E9}
        \definecolor{darkgray}{HTML}{333333}
        \definecolor{mediumgray}{HTML}{666666}
        \definecolor{lightgray}{HTML}{F5F5F5}

        \usepackage{titlesec}
        \titleformat{\section}
          {\color{oiblue}\normalfont\Large\bfseries}
          {\thesection}{0em}{}[\color{oiblue}\titlerule]
        \titleformat{\subsection}
          {\color{darkgray}\normalfont\large\bfseries}
          {}{0em}{}
        \titlespacing*{\section}{0pt}{12pt}{6pt}
        \titlespacing*{\subsection}{0pt}{8pt}{4pt}

        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \usepackage{array}
        \usepackage{colortbl}
        \arrayrulecolor{mediumgray}
        \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.3}
        \rowcolors{2}{white}{lightgray}

        \usepackage{tcolorbox}
        \tcbuselibrary{skins,breakable}

        \newtcolorbox{stepbox}[1][]{
          colback=oiskyblue!8,
          colframe=oiblue,
          fonttitle=\bfseries\large,
          coltitle=oiblue,
          colbacktitle=white,
          title=#1,
          boxrule=1.5pt,
          arc=4pt,
          toptitle=3pt,
          bottomtitle=3pt,
          left=8pt,
          right=8pt,
          breakable
        }

        \newtcolorbox{tipbox}[1][]{
          colback=oigreen!8,
          colframe=oigreen,
          colbacktitle=oigreen,
          coltitle=white,
          fonttitle=\bfseries,
          title=#1,
          boxrule=1pt,
          arc=3pt,
          breakable
        }

        \newtcolorbox{warnbox}[1][]{
          colback=oiorange!8,
          colframe=oiorange,
          colbacktitle=oiorange,
          coltitle=white,
          fonttitle=\bfseries,
          title=#1,
          boxrule=1pt,
          arc=3pt,
          breakable
        }

        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \setlist[enumerate]{leftmargin=*, itemsep=4pt, parsep=2pt}
        \setlist[itemize]{leftmargin=*, itemsep=2pt, parsep=2pt}
---

\begin{center}
{\LARGE\bfseries\color{oiblue} EpiFlow D3 --- Quick Start Guide}\\[6pt]
{\large Analyze your spectral flow cytometry data in 10 minutes}\\[4pt]
{\color{mediumgray}\url{https://epiflow.serranolab.org}}
\end{center}

\vspace{6pt}

\begin{stepbox}[Step 1: Prepare Your Data]

Your data needs to be in \texttt{.rds} format. Use the \textbf{OMIQ $\rightarrow$ EpiFlow Converter} to convert your OMIQ CSV export:

\begin{center}
\textcolor{oiblue}{\url{https://serranolab.shinyapps.io/File_EpiFlow_converter/}}
\end{center}

\textbf{What the converter does:}
\begin{itemize}
\item Extracts metadata (genotype, replicate) from OMIQ filenames automatically
\item Detects H3-PTM and phenotypic marker columns
\item \textbf{Gates cell cycle phases} (G0/G1, S, G2, G2/M, M) using DNA content with flexible thresholding per group
\item Transforms and exports your data in EpiFlow-compatible long format
\end{itemize}

\textbf{Quick converter workflow:}
\begin{itemize}
\item Open the converter (link above or locally in RStudio/Positron)
\item Upload your OMIQ CSV export
\item Map columns and edit file metadata
\item Configure cell cycle gating (DNA channel, threshold method, optional phH3 for M-phase)
\item Click Process $\rightarrow$ Download \texttt{.rds}
\end{itemize}
\end{stepbox}

\vspace{4pt}

\begin{stepbox}[Step 2: Upload and Explore]

\begin{enumerate}
\item Go to \textcolor{oiblue}{\textbf{https://epiflow.serranolab.org}}
\item Click \textbf{Choose .rds File} and upload your file
\item The \textbf{Overview} tab loads automatically --- check:
  \begin{itemize}
  \item Are all genotypes present?
  \item Are replicates balanced?
  \item Does cell cycle distribution look reasonable?
  \item Do marker box plots show expected ranges?
  \end{itemize}
\item Try \textbf{Ridge Plots}: select a marker, see how distributions differ between groups
\item Try \textbf{Violin Plots}: same data, different view --- good for comparing medians
\end{enumerate}
\end{stepbox}

\vspace{4pt}

\begin{stepbox}[Step 3: Run Statistics]

\begin{enumerate}
\item Go to the \textbf{Statistics} tab
\item Click \textbf{Run All Markers} --- this fits a linear mixed model for every H3-PTM
\item The model accounts for biological replicates: \texttt{value $\sim$ group + (1|replicate)}
\item Review results:
  \begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{Forest Plot}: effect sizes with confidence intervals across all markers
  \item \textbf{Volcano Plot}: significance vs.\ fold-change --- top corners are the hits
  \item \textbf{Violin Plots}: now show p-values and effect sizes on each plot
  \end{itemize}
\item \textbf{Stratify by cell cycle}: re-run with ``Stratify by $\rightarrow$ Cell Cycle'' to find phase-specific effects
\end{enumerate}

\begin{tipbox}[What to report]
For each significant marker, report: the \textbf{adjusted p-value} (FDR-corrected), the \textbf{Cohen's d} effect size, and the \textbf{direction} of change. Include the forest plot and representative violin plots in your figures.
\end{tipbox}
\end{stepbox}

\vspace{4pt}

\begin{stepbox}[Step 4: Go Deeper (Optional)]

Pick the analyses that match your question:

\begin{center}
\rowcolors{2}{white}{lightgray}
\begin{tabular}{p{4cm}p{9cm}}
\toprule
\textbf{I want to\ldots} & \textbf{Use this tab} \\
\midrule
See if groups separate in marker space & \textbf{PCA} or \textbf{UMAP} (Dimensionality Reduction) \\
Find which markers best distinguish groups & \textbf{Random Forest} (ML tab) --- check feature importance \\
Discover co-regulation changes & \textbf{Correlation} --- run differential correlation \\
Determine marker positivity rates & \textbf{Positivity} --- GMM thresholding per replicate \\
Gate on two markers & \textbf{Quadrant Gating} --- define populations, filter by them \\
Find cell subpopulations & \textbf{Clustering} --- k-means, Louvain, or Leiden \\
Phase-specific analysis & \textbf{Cell Cycle} tab --- per-phase marker comparisons \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}
\end{stepbox}

\vspace{4pt}

\begin{stepbox}[Step 5: Export Your Results]

\textbf{Figures:} Every plot has SVG and PNG (2x) export buttons in the top-right corner. Use SVG for publications (vector, editable).

\textbf{Multi-panel figures:} Use the \textbf{Figure Composer} to assemble panels with automatic A/B/C labels.

\textbf{Statistics:} Every result table has a \textbf{Download CSV} button.

\textbf{Full report:} Click \textbf{Generate Report} for an HTML document with all visualizations.

\textbf{Reproducibility:} Click \textbf{Save Session} to download a JSON file. Anyone can \textbf{Load Session} to reproduce your exact analysis.
\end{stepbox}

\vspace{8pt}

\begin{warnbox}[Common Mistakes to Avoid]
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{Don't use t-tests} on single-cell data --- cells from the same replicate are not independent. EpiFlow's LMM handles this correctly.
\item \textbf{Don't skip filtering} --- always check Overview first. Garbage in = garbage out.
\item \textbf{Don't ignore effect sizes} --- a tiny p-value with Cohen's d $<$ 0.2 is not biologically meaningful.
\item \textbf{Don't forget to save your session} --- the server doesn't persist data between visits.
\item \textbf{Always report adjusted p-values} --- EpiFlow corrects for multiple testing (Benjamini-Hochberg). Never report raw p-values.
\end{itemize}
\end{warnbox}

\vspace{8pt}

\begin{tipbox}[Recommended First Analysis Checklist]
\begin{enumerate}
\item[$\square$] Upload .rds and check Overview
\item[$\square$] Run All Markers LMM
\item[$\square$] Screenshot the Forest Plot
\item[$\square$] Check top hits in Violin Plots
\item[$\square$] Run PCA or UMAP to see group separation
\item[$\square$] Run Random Forest --- does ML agree with statistics?
\item[$\square$] Export figures (SVG) and tables (CSV)
\item[$\square$] Save Session (JSON)
\end{enumerate}
\end{tipbox}

\vspace{10pt}

\begin{center}
\textcolor{mediumgray}{\footnotesize
\textbf{EpiFlow D3} | Serrano Lab, Center for Regenerative Medicine (CReM), Boston University\\
\url{https://serranolab.github.io/online/} | AGPL-3.0 License | \url{https://github.com/SerranoLab/epiflow-d3}
}
\end{center}
